items.comment: See how messy this display is due to layering and overlapping white boxes and the arrow location (should be up to the start of the icon)
checks: Graph labels and label backgrounds must not obscure the primary selected node.
Repeated relationship stacks must collapse into readable count-bearing nodes or rows.
Arrowheads must terminate at the visual boundary of the target icon rather than running through the icon centre.
Relationship labels must remain readable without overlapping unrelated node labels.
The relationship drawer must support inspection without covering the main graph target.

items.comment: Search for osdatahub.os.uk returns records, but the graph context collapses into an Ordnance Survey fan-in with poor labels instead of a readable graph for the searched target.
checks: Switching from search results to Graph must preserve the selected or highlighted search-result route.
Dense search-result graphs must explain any grouping dimension in visible text.
Record nodes in a dense search context should be grouped by a meaningful dimension before labels overlap.
Relationship rows must remain available without covering the graph target.
Graph legends and captions must distinguish real corpus records from generated grouping stacks.

items.comment: OS Data Hub cluster remains unreadable after zoom because close record stacks, labels and arrows converge on the selected host node.
checks: Zoom must not be the only remedy for dense graph clusters.
Dense record clusters should collapse into count-bearing stacks by record type or another visible grouping dimension.
Clicking one grouped stack should expand that group while re-stacking any previously expanded group.
Labels for the selected host or provider must remain readable at the default zoom.
Arrowheads must terminate at the target icon boundary and avoid the selected-node label.

items.comment: After searching OS, selecting OS Maps API, switching to Graph and double-clicking OS Data Hub, the Record type facet can look like a false breakdown unless graph grouping and reduction context are explicit.
checks: Double-clicking metadata graph nodes such as host, provider, format, topic or licence must reduce the active context where that is a facet value.
Facet counts must describe the active search/filter reduction rather than an unexplained graph-only selection.
When graph nodes are grouped, the UI must show the grouping dimension and expanded group.
Record type labels must use OKF concept terms consistently with the bundle specification.
The left panel must keep selected facet values visible after the graph changes context.
